evals.expected_output: When a git diff is present, dispatches to session review, triages changed files, verifies citation anchors, validates non-trivial findings with evidence, and stops at an approval gate before fixes.
assertions: Detects active diff before choosing mode
Does not run a full audit implicitly

evals.expected_output: When no changed files are present, shows a mode menu and requires the explicit audit keyword before a full repository audit.
assertions: Offers audit, scoped, PR, source, simplify, history, delta, and learnings modes

evals.expected_output: Dispatches to scoped review of the path, reads the target, applies relevant lenses, verifies line citations, and reports findings with severity, confidence, reasoning, evidence, and recommendations.
Runs full repository audit only because audit is explicit, using triage, non-overlapping reviewer ownership, specialist lenses, judge reconciliation, and an approval-gated report.
assertions: Uses parallel lanes when scope warrants

evals.expected_output: Recognizes PR #42, reviews PR changes and intent, and formats findings as Conventional Comments while preserving evidence, confidence, and citation requirements.
Recognizes the git range, reviews changed files in that range, and emits SARIF v2.1-compatible findings using verified anchors.
assertions: Does not use format as scope

evals.expected_output: Dispatches to scoped path review for src/auth/session.py and treats each --format flag only as an output modifier; emits or describes SARIF v2.1 and Conventional Comments output without changing the selected scope or mode, without treating format values as targets, and without inferring PR, range, session, or audit behavior from the format flags.
assertions: Keeps scoped path selection independent from format flags
Does not use format values as scope or mode
Does not infer PR, range, session, or audit from output format

evals.expected_output: Treats --format sarif only as an output preference, then chooses the normal empty-argument dispatch path: session review when git diff has changed files, or the no-diff mode menu when clean. It does not invent a SARIF review mode, target the word sarif, or start a full audit.
assertions: Uses empty-argument dispatch after parsing format
Does not use format as scope or mode
Does not start an implicit full audit

evals.expected_output: Runs read-only simplification analysis, states behavior invariants and semantic-change boundaries, and reports only behavior-preserving opportunities without editing.
Applies simplification only after target, intent, behavior invariants, validation basis, and scope-risk gates pass; otherwise asks or falls back to analysis.
Explains why the target is complex and how to simplify it safely without editing files.
assertions: Separates safe simplifications from risky changes

evals.expected_output: Classifies source reputation, source-list evidence needs, install syntax, dedupe risk, provenance needs, and the next trust-gate step without installing.

Dispatches specifically to source commands mode, reads candidate metadata and files only as needed, extracts and classifies executable surfaces such as hooks, scripts, executable bits, package-manager commands, shell snippets, backtick commands, command substitutions, allowed-tools, network or credential access, and filesystem writes without running candidate code, installing, syncing, vendoring, or issuing a promotion decision.
assertions: Classifies executable surfaces and risk categories
Does not execute candidate scripts or package commands
Does not install, sync, vendor, or emit a promotion decision

evals.expected_output: Dispatches to source inspect mode, reads local source files, frontmatter, hooks, scripts, references, command substitutions, and bundled assets for review evidence without running candidate code or installing the source.
assertions: Does not execute or install candidate code

evals.expected_output: Dispatches to source provenance mode and checks source owner, URL, license, commit or hash, source-list consistency, access date, and trust evidence before any source decision or install recommendation.
assertions: Checks owner, license, and commit or hash evidence
Does not install or promote without provenance evidence

evals.expected_output: Runs or describes the static source audit helper, interprets JSON findings, and treats script output as a first-pass signal rather than final approval.
Produces one install-now, inspect-then-install, keep-global-only, build-locally, or avoid decision with owner, license, provenance, executable-surface, credential, network, and dedupe evidence.
Overlays the security lens on the scoped review, checking auth, secrets, data exposure, injection, trust boundaries, and exploitability with verified evidence.
assertions: Does not widen scope without evidence
Reports security findings through standard finding contract

evals.expected_output: Checks dependency provenance, slopsquatting, scripts, lockfile drift, version pinning, and executable package surfaces without installing packages.
Dispatches to stored review delta mode, compares current and previous review records, and reports new, resolved, and recurring findings without mixing in fix-report sections.
Dispatches to read-only history mode, lists stored review records for the requested project, and does not mix in a new review, delta comparison, or fix pass.
assertions: Does not start a new review or fix pass

evals.expected_output: Dispatches to learnings list mode, reads false-positive review learnings, reports matching scope or project metadata, and does not add, clear, or apply suppressions unless the user explicitly selects that learnings subcommand.
assertions: Does not mutate learnings without explicit add or clear

evals.expected_output: Treats fix as a post-review pass requiring selected approved finding IDs, loads the auto-fix protocol, previews diffs, requests explicit approval if not already given, and verifies after edits.

evals.prompt: Install /review across Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Grok, Cursor, and Gemini CLI.
evals.expected_output: Explains that review uses portable prompt-first frontmatter, no root model override, no skill-scoped hooks by default, and validates harness behavior through wagents skills sync dry-runs rather than assuming one harness's semantics.
Honors the explicit scoped mode, reviews only the requested target unless evidence requires a bounded dependency read, and reports findings through the standard finding contract.
assertions: Does not widen to full audit

evals.expected_output: Runs scoped review and source/provenance review as separate read-only lanes, collects lane-local artifacts, and merges only through Judge-assigned canonical RV IDs.
assertions: Uses Judge for dedupe and canonical IDs

evals.expected_output: Runs the scoped review lane but does not infer or execute fix behavior from mixed modes without selected approved findings and explicit edit approval.
For browser-grounded web-quality review, defaults to the repo-managed Chrome DevTools MCP attached-browser path, reports degraded mode if unavailable, and avoids adding a duplicate browser owner.
assertions: Does not create duplicate MCP/browser config

evals.expected_output: Dispatches to scoped review with the skill-assets lens, applies skill-creator structural gates such as dispatch table, reference index, critical rules, canonical vocabulary, scope boundaries, progressive disclosure, eval coverage, package portability, and generated docs/catalog consistency, and remains read-only unless approved finding IDs are supplied.
assertions: Does not edit without approved findings

evals.expected_output: Recognizes the skill directory as a skill asset, runs scoped review with the skill-assets lens attached by default, checks skill-creator audit and packaging evidence when available, and does not widen to all skills or run live installs.
assertions: Keeps review scoped to the requested skill
Avoids live installs and sync apply

evals.expected_output: Scans source surfaces without executing candidate code, redacts secret-like excerpts, avoids broad KEY substring false positives, and treats output as first-pass evidence.

evals.prompt: /review check this thing and maybe fix whatever is wrong
evals.expected_output: Treats the request as unrecognized or ambiguous, performs no review or fix implementation, does not infer edit approval from maybe fix, and asks one concise scope/mode question that gets the user to choose a reviewable target or mode and confirm whether approved finding IDs exist before any edit-capable path.
assertions: Does not review, fix, or edit from ambiguous input

evals.prompt: /review build a new login page and wire it to the API
evals.expected_output: Negative control: identifies feature implementation as out of scope for /review, refuses to build or wire the login page, does not create or edit files, and asks for an existing diff, PR, path, or approved finding ID to review or for the user to make a separate implementation request outside review mode.
assertions: Negative control should not trigger implementation
Does not create, edit, or propose code for the feature
Keeps /review boundary instead of running fix or simplify apply
Requests a concrete reviewable artifact before continuing
